text:
It was a quiet night. The shadows were long, the wind whispered through the trees, and somewhere deep in the belly of the night, dogs howled. At the edge of town, under a moonlit sky, there lay a small, solitary house. The porch swings creaked as the wind blew them again, and a garden light flickered. Our story begins with someone settling into the cozy warmth of the living room. They lean back in their chair, arms stretched out, eyes closed, the distant chirping of birds the only sound breaking the silence.

Deep into rest, they are suddenly jolted awake by an urgent knocking at the door. Opening one eye, they peer through the window shade into the street. There, in a circle formed by the light of a distant street lamp, stands an unfamiliar man. He holds a hand mirror up, peering at his own face. The clock says it's around midnight. They exchange a brief but intense glance – a silent conversation
